title: taraxacum palustre var. udum
intro: Taraxacum palustre var. udum, also known as marsh dandelion or water dandelion, is a type of flowering plant in the dandelion genus, characterized by its yellow flowers and leafy stems. The plant has been used for its medicinal properties, including its potential to reduce inflammation, improve digestion, and support liver health, due to its high content of flavonoids and other bioactive compounds. Marsh dandelion is believed to have various health benefits, such as reducing oxidative stress, protecting against certain diseases, and promoting overall well-being. In herbal medicine, the plant is often prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and is also used in traditional remedies to treat various ailments, including liver and gallbladder issues.
